---
title: "IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School Day 1: Logical Neural Networks (Makondo)"
description: "LNN — single model for neural and logical, weighted Łukasiewicz logic, sound and complete, gradient-based optimization."
author: "synesis"
date: "2022-08-09"
categories: [NLP, knowledge graphs, conference, paper]
image: cover.jpg
---

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![IBM Neuro-Symbolic AI Summer School 2022. Image from [IBM Research](https://ibm.github.io/neuro-symbolic-ai/events/ns-summerschool2022/).](cover.jpg)

![Slide from Makondo's talk at the [IBM Research](https://ibm.github.io/neuro-symbolic-ai/events/ns-summerschool2022/).](02.jpg)

![Slide from Makondo's talk at the [IBM Research](https://ibm.github.io/neuro-symbolic-ai/events/ns-summerschool2022/).](03.jpg)

![Slide from Makondo's talk at the [IBM Research](https://ibm.github.io/neuro-symbolic-ai/events/ns-summerschool2022/).](04.jpg)
:::
IBM NeuroSymbolicAI Summer School 2022 Day 1: Logical Neural Networks (LNN) given by Ndivhuwo MAKONDO:

* One single model for both neural and logical representations
* Weighted Łukasiewicz (real-valued) logic
* Proven to be sound and strongly complete
* Upward/downward (reverse) inference
* Uncertainty bounds allow representations of "don't-know" and contradiction
* Gradient-based optimization

Ryan Riegel, Alexander Gray, Francois Luus, Naweed Khan, Ndivhuwo MAKONDO, Ismail Yunus Akhalwaya, Haifeng Qian, et al. 2020. "Logical Neural Networks." arXiv [cs.AI]. arXiv. <http://arxiv.org/abs/2006.13155>.

Summer School site: <https://ibm.github.io/neuro-symbolic-ai/events/ns-summerschool2022/>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_ibm-neurosymbolicai-logic-activity-6962891812650831872-yAfM).*
